revision.comment: Text replacement - "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure" to "there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |Dimensions=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |Coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == Size == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |Dimensions=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |Coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |Map2={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == Size == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}

{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == Size == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. {{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == Size == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |Dimensions=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |Coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |map2={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == Size == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|Burial=" to "|Burial Facility="
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |Dimensions=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |Coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |map2={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == Size == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}

{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |Dimensions=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |map={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == area == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |area=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |map={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == area == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}

Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == area == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World Cultural Heritage Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / Cultural Heritage Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == area == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World other_designation Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / other_designation Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |part_of={{ill|Mozu Kofun part_of|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |area=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |map={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun part_of]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == area == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World other_designation Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / other_designation Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun part_of - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] |name=Tatsusayama Kofun |image=[[File:Tatsusayama Kofun 006.jpg|280px]] |Caption=Rear mound of Tatsusayama Kofun |location=[[Osaka Prefecture]], [[Sakai City]], [[Sakai Ward]], Daisen Town |Group={{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |type=[[Keyhole-shaped kofun]] |Period=Mid-5th century |area=61 m in mound length; rear mound height 8 m |burial goods=Cylindrical haniwa, lid-shaped haniwa |coordinates={{Coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tatsusayama Kofun}} |map={{location map|Japan Osaka Prefecture|relief=1|label=Tatsusayama
Kofun|lat_deg=34|lat_min=33|lat_sec=40.4|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=00.3|marker=place-of-worship|mark=帆立貝形古墳-西北西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=210|caption=location in Osaka Prefecture}}{{Switcher |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=16|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show nearby map |{{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|34|33|40.4|N|135|29|00.3|E}}}} |Show wider map }} |Burial Facility=Unknown |HistoricSite=Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun / Designated by Sakai City |Remarks=Most of the mound is under the jurisdiction of the Imperial Household Agency; the foothills and moat are managed by Sakai City. |AccompanyingTombs=Rogō Detached Area, Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku }} [[File:Tatsusayama_Kofun_Aerial_photograph_2007.jpg|thumb|Aerial view of Tatsusayama Kofun (2007)
{{National Land Aerial Photograph}}]] Tatsusayama Kofun (竜佐山古墳) is a [[kofun]] located in Daisen Nakamachi, [[Sakai Ward]], [[Sakai City]], [[Osaka Prefecture]], within Daisen Park. It is part of the [[Mozu Kofun Group]] and considered an accompanying tomb of the [[Daisenryō Kofun]] (Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku). It is registered as part of the [[Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group|Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan]], a [[World Heritage Site]] for cultural properties. == Overview == The keyhole-shaped kofun, oriented with its front section to the west, lies near the outer moat on the southern side of the Daisenryō Kofun. Its axis is parallel to the southern outer moat of the Daisenryō Kofun{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/911/00000146 |title=Tatsusayama Kofun / World Heritage Component / National Cultural Properties Database |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. Historical maps from 1730 refer to it as "Ryōsayama," and early Shōwa period records indicate it was once called "Ryūsayama"{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/hakubutsukan/collection/mozukofungun/kofun.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun - Imperial Detached Area "Rogō" - Daisen Nakamachi / Imperial Tomb of Emperor Nintoku Kofun Encyclopedia |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Museum}}. The mound measures 61 m in length, with the rear mound measuring 43 m in diameter and 8 m in height. The front section is 26 m wide and 2 m high{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}. == area == The dimensions of the kofun are as follows{{Sfn|Kofun Cluster|2014|p=21}}: * Mound Length: 61 m * Rear Mound: ** Diameter: 43 m ** Height: 8 m * Front Section: ** Width: 26 m ** Height: 2 m == Cultural Properties == === Sakai City Designated Historic Site === Designation: Moat of Tatsusayama Kofun. Designated on April 11, 2016{{Cite web|url=https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/shokai/bunya/shiseki/tatsusayama.html |title=Tatsusayama Kofun Moat |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section}}. == World Heritage == On July 6, 2019, the kofun was registered as part of the World other_designation Site Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan{{Cite web|url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/special_content/hlinkJ |title=World Heritage / other_designation Online |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}{{Cite web|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/901/00000020 |title=World Heritage / Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group - Ancient Kofun Clusters of Japan |accessdate=2022-08-04 |publisher=Cultural Affairs Agency}}. == Access == * By train: Approximately 11 minutes on foot from [[JR]] [[Hanwa Line]] [[Uenoshiba Station]] * By bus: From [[Nankai Kōya Line]] [[Sakai Higashi Station]], take a bus to Horiue Ryokumachi Itchō or Higashiyama Shako-mae and get off at Nankyō-dō Yonchō. Walk 5 minutes. == References == {{Citation help}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|title=Mozu Kofun Cluster - Cultural Properties of Sakai, 7th Edition|year=2014|publisher=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|author=Sakai City Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section Editorial Office|ref={{SfnRef|Kofun Cluster|2014}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 1|date=2008-03-31|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Lifelong Learning Section Cultural Property Division|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 1|2008}}}} * {{Cite book|title=Survey of Mozu Kofun Cluster Vol. 2|year=2009|publisher=Sakai City Board of Education|editor=Sakai City Mayor's Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section|ref={{SfnRef|Survey Vol. 2|2009}}}} {{Commonscat|Tatsusayama Kofun}} == External Links == * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/smph/kanko/rekishi/bunkazai/bunkazai/isekishokai/kangotoshi.html Sakai Moat Town Relics] - Sakai City Office Cultural Affairs Bureau Cultural Property Section * [https://www.sakai-tcb.or.jp/spot/detail/104 Tatsusayama Kofun] - Sakai Navi (Sakai Tourism Convention Association) * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kanko/rekishi/sei/index.html World Heritage: Mozu-Furuichi Kofun Group] - Sakai City Office * [https://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kurashi/koen/shokai/daisenkouen.html Daisen Park] - Sakai City Office [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of Sakai Ward]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]]==See Also== Scallop-shaped Kofun * {{ill|Ryusa-yama Kofun|ja|竜佐山古墳}}
